rulings.Case Title: TAYLOR KOROBOW VS. SUSAN HARDIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 23, 2008 LINE 3. PLAINTIFF TAYLOR KOROBOW'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint IS DENIED AS TO DEFENDANT DOUGLAS ROSS CONSTRUCTION ("D.R.C."). THE COURT HAS GRANTED D.R.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND THE AMENDMENT, THEREFORE, APPEARS TO BE UNTIMELY. ALSO PLAINTIFF FAILS TO EXPLAIN THE DELAY IN SEEKING TO AMEND. FINALLY, THERE DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE A LEGAL BASIS TO HOLD A GENERAL CONTRACTOR TO STRICT LIABILITY WHERE ANOTHER PARTY IS THE ADMITTED DEVELOPER. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 23, 2008 LINE 14. DEFENDANT ARCH INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT RULED ON JANUARY 3, 2008 THAT ALAMEDA COUNTY HAS JURISDICTION OF THIS DISPUTE AND THAT THE CITY'S CLAIMS ARE A MANDATORY CROSS-COMPLAINT IN THAT ACTION. FOR THAT REASON, THIS DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LELAND PORTEOUS DDS et al VS. LAWRENCE PORTEOUS DDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Judgment;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 23, 2008 LINE 15. PETITIONERS LELAND PORTEOUS AND, LELAND F. PORTEOUS, D.D.S., P.C.'S ('DR. LEE') Motion To Enforce Judgment: Hearing required. The Court's tentative ruling is to refer the matter to the arbitrator, the Honorable Daniel Hanlon (Ret.), to seek his understanding of paragraph 2(b) of the final reward. Specifically, Justice Hanlon, first, is respectfully requested to clarify whether the intent of the award was to require the parties "to accept the obligations of the partnership agreement or forfeit his right of sale" regardless of the reason(s) for the failure to "sign a conforming Contract of Sale"; second, was it contemplated that the Court or the arbitrator would assess the conduct of the parties during the 30 day period to determine whether one or the other was acting in good faith and if so, what would be the consequence of a failure to act in good faith; third, was the Court or the arbitrator expected to parse the parties' exchange of "contracts" to assess whether one or both or collectively, one could discern a "conforming Contract of Sale"; and fourth, assuming the parties failed to sign a "conforming Contract of Sale", what is meant by the "obligations of the partnership agreement" and the "forfeiture" of the "right of sale." Finally to carry out the intent of the award, any other guidance of Justice Hanlon is most welcome. The parties are to provide Justice Hanlon only with the pleadings prepared in connection with this motion and the parties' answers to the Court's questions. The matter shall be continued for 45 days to receive Justice Hanlon's response. =(302/PJM/AY)
